intro: Rhipidoglossum peglerae, commonly known as the peglera orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its unique, epiphytic growth habit and small, white flowers. The peglera orchid has been traditionally used in African folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhipidoglossum peglerae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the peglera orchid is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal teas, tonics, and capsules.
